# Let's read the provided file
path = "/mnt/data/file-VaynBkvkLAH6SnyjkTHrPf-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:5000]

text = open('/mnt/data/file-VaynBkvkLAH6SnyjkTHrPf-text_to_analyse.txt','r',encoding='utf-8').read()

open_br = '﴿'
close_br = '﴾'

spans = []

i = 0
while True:
    i = text.find(open_br, i)
    if i == -1:
        break
    start_inner = i + 1
    # find nearest closing bracket after i
    j = text.find(close_br, start_inner)
    if j == -1:
        # No closing bracket. Use end of line or end of text.
        # Find newline after start_inner
        nl = text.find('\n', start_inner)
        if nl == -1:
            end_pos = len(text)
        else:
            end_pos = nl
        raw_inner = text[start_inner:end_pos]
        # trim punctuation we must exclude at edges? The rule says exclude punctuation, quotes, bracket etc.
        # Here there may be leading spaces? Not likely. We'll strip whitespace.
        stripped = raw_inner.strip()
        # compute trimmed indices
        lead_strip = len(raw_inner) - len(raw_inner.lstrip())
        trail_strip = len(raw_inner) - len(raw_inner.rstrip())
        # Additional: if trailing punctuation like commas? In this case none.
        span_start = start_inner + lead_strip
        span_end = end_pos - trail_strip
        spans.append((span_start, span_end, text[span_start:span_end]))
        i = start_inner  # move forward
    else:
        raw_inner = text[start_inner:j]
        # trim surrounding whitespace
        lead_strip = len(raw_inner) - len(raw_inner.lstrip())
        trail_strip = len(raw_inner) - len(raw_inner.rstrip())
        span_start = start_inner + lead_strip
        span_end = j - trail_strip
        spans.append((span_start, span_end, text[span_start:span_end]))
        i = j + 1

len(spans), spans[:10]

spans = []

i = 0
while True:
    i = text.find('﴿', i)
    if i == -1:
        break
    start_inner = i + 1
    # find next newline after start_inner
    nl = text.find('\n', start_inner)
    if nl == -1:
        nl = len(text)
    # find next closing bracket after start_inner
    j = text.find('﴾', start_inner)
    if j != -1 and j < nl:
        end_pos = j
    else:
        end_pos = nl
    raw_inner = text[start_inner:end_pos]
    # trim whitespace on both sides
    lead_strip = len(raw_inner) - len(raw_inner.lstrip())
    trail_strip = len(raw_inner) - len(raw_inner.rstrip())
    span_start = start_inner + lead_strip
    span_end = end_pos - trail_strip
    spans.append((span_start, span_end, text[span_start:span_end]))
    i = end_pos + 1

len(spans), spans
